message.content: Process Injection via Ptrace System Calls involves adversaries injecting malicious code into processes to evade defenses and potentially elevate privileges. This technique leverages the ptrace system call, which allows a debugging process to observe and control another process, including altering memory and register values. Common methods include writing arbitrary code into a running process and using PTRACE_SETREGS to set the next instruction or PTRACE_POKETEXT/PTRACE_POKEDATA to copy data to specific memory addresses. This method can mask malicious activity under legitimate processes, making detection challenging. However, it may not work on non-child or higher-privileged processes. The impact includes unauthorized access to process memory, system, and network resources, and potential privilege escalation. Mitigation strategies include restricting ptrace usage to privileged users, employing strict access controls, and using security monitoring tools to detect unusual process behaviors.
